trails.name: Westmere walkway
trails.description: Westmere Walkway is located to the north of Whanganui. It can be accessed from its northern entrance at Brunswick Road or from its southern end at Kelvin Street.

trails.name: Marton Sash and Door Tramline Cycleway

OLD BLYTH TRACK
trails.description: Follow this historic trail through red beech and kaikawaka forests to connect with the Waitonga Falls and Round the Mountain tracks.

trails.name: WAIPAKIHI RIVER TO MT URCHIN TK
Thunderbolt Track
trails.description: A popular access route for hunters, from Waipakihi River to the Thunderbolt tops.

trails.name: Kiko Loop Track
trails.description: This short loop track is a great way for walkers and mountain bikers to experience the variety of nature in the Kaimanawa Forest Park.

trails.name: Waipakihi Middle Range Route
trails.description: An access route from the upper Waipakihi valley to the Middle Range tops.

trails.name: Tongariro River Anglers True Left Trks
Tongariro River Anglers True Right Trks
Marton Sash and Door Tramline cycleway
Atene Skyline Track
trails.description: The Atene Skyline Track has spectacular views and a campsite for trampers wanting to stay the night.

trails.name: Bruce Park Loop Track
trails.description: An easy walk through a podocarp and broadleaf forest remnant with impressive specimens of rimu, totara and kahikatea. It's a great walk for kids.

trails.name: Kaimanawa Road short walks
trails.description: Two family friendly short walks starting from Urchin Campsite at the end of Kaimanawa Road.

trails.name: Mangaweka Scenic Reserve Track
trails.description: Enjoy great views over the Rangitikei River terraces on this short loop walk.

trails.name: Mt Urchin Track
trails.description: Mount Urchin offers an easily accessible wilderness of beautiful beech forest, incredible views and a real alpine environment.

trails.name: Ngapuketurua Track
trails.description: This track begins on the Kiko Loop and continues as far as the park boundary on the slopes of Mt Ngapuketurua.

trails.name: Ohakune Old Coach Road
trails.description: Take a trip back into New Zealand's rail heritage while you walk or cycle an old coach road. The road was used to carry passengers and goods between two railheads on the North Island main trunk link before they were linked in 1908.

trails.name: Rotopounamu Track
trails.description: This track circles a beautiful lake surrounded by native forest and is a special favourite of tree lovers, birdwatchers, and families.

trails.name: Tongariro Alpine Crossing
trails.description: Trek across a volcanic alpine landscape of dramatic contrasts. From May to October, alpine skills are essential for this track – it's best to go with a guide.

trails.name: Tree Trunk Gorge tracks
trails.description: Walk or mountain bike through the Kaimanawa Forest Park taking in two spectacular gorges, and the characteristic beech and kamahi forest.

trails.name: Urchin and Umukarikari tracks
trails.description: Climb above the bush line to the exposed alpine ridge of Umukarikari and Mount Urchin. Enjoy fantastic views and access the Waipakihi valley and hut.

trails.name: Waipakihi River tracks
trails.description: Head up the Waipakihi River Valley, a popular option for hunters to access the tops, or a great day out for swimming in the summer.

trails.name: Zekes Hut Track
trails.description: An ideal day hike or easy overnight tramp to a newly replaced hut nestled within Hihitahi Forest Sanctuary north of Taihape.

trails.name: Whakapapa Village short walks
trails.description: These short walks are either accessible on foot from Whakapapa Village or require a short drive to the start of the track.

trails.name: Ohakune short walks
trails.description: Explore the forest on these short walks in Ohakune.

trails.name: Taranaki Falls Walk
trails.description: Walk to Taranaki Falls (20 m) and enjoy excellent mountain views and a range of land forms on a loop track. This track is part of Te Araroa Trail.

trails.name: Gordon Park Loop Track
trails.description: This flat and well-formed track passes through native podocarp-hardwood forest and has a variety of birds - with a picnic table at the start, it's a good option for families.

trails.name: Tongariro Alpine Crossing Track
trails.description: Day Hike

trails.name: Ohakune Old Coach Road
trails.description: Take a trip back into New Zealand's rail heritage while you walk or cycle an old coach road. The road was used to carry passengers and goods between two railheads on the North Island main trunk link before they were linked in 1908.

trails.name: Ridge Track
trails.description: This walk rewards the effort of a short climb with panoramic views of Mt Ngauruhoe and the surrounding area.

trails.name: Silica Rapids Walk
trails.description: Experience a range of vegetation types as well as the creamy-white Silica Rapid terraces.

trails.name: Soda Springs Walk
trails.description: Walk to cold water springs that create a small oasis of flowers in summer.

trails.name: Southern Access Corridor
trails.description: This route, bordering New Zealand Defence Force land, heads from the Desert Road through to the Otamateanui Stream and Rangitikei River confluence.

trails.name: Tama Lakes Track
trails.description: A great day hike with views of Lower Tama Lake, Taranaki Falls, Mt Ruapehu and Mt Ngauruhoe.

trails.name: Taurewa Track
trails.description: Follow old milling trails through the bush.

trails.name: Tupapakurua Falls Track
trails.description: Walk to a lookout point with views of Mt Taranaki and Mt Ruapehu, then on to the remote Tupapakurua Falls.

trails.name: Waitonga Falls Track
trails.description: The 39 m Waitonga Falls is Tongariro National Park’s highest waterfall. The well-formed track passes through mountain beech and kaikawaka forest.

trails.name: Whakapapaiti Valley Track
trails.description: Winding through stunted beech forest, the Whakapapaiti Valley Track makes its way to the Whakapapaiti Hut. From here you can continue in a loop back to Whakapapa Village. This track is part of Te Araroa Trail.

trails.name: Whakapapanui Walk
trails.description: Follow Whakapapanui Stream and enjoy the shelter of the forest.

trails.name: Historic Waihohonu Hut Track
trails.description: Walk to the historic Waihohonu Hut, built in 1903/04 and used as a stopover for stage coaches.

trails.name: Lake Rotokura Walk
trails.description: Experience lakes, beech forest and plentiful bird life feature on this short walk.

trails.name: Jubilee Park Walkway
trails.description: Part of this track includes the various pathways through the Ohakune Carrot Adventure Park, which features the famous giant carrot, free BBQ areas, exercise equipment, a playground, bike tracks, gardens and paths beside the Mangateitei Stream. Three diff

trails.name: Tupapakurua Falls Track
trails.description: Walk to a lookout point with views of Mt Taranaki and Mt Ruapehu, then on to the remote Tupapakurua Falls.
